observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
require the Municipal
Accountant to cancel the stale
checks and restore the balances
of the corresponding cash in
bank and liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were recorded in the books.
observations.reason: Not all stale checks were adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) update the
Property Cards based on the
result of the physical count; (ii)
issue demand letters to each
accountable officer/personnel
for the production of the
missing PPE he/she is
accountable for; (iii)
investigate the causes of the
missing PPE items to
identify the persons deemed
liable therefor; (iv) locate the
LTO Certificate of Registration
which were not yet presented
for verification of the AT; and
(v) cause the renewal of
registration with the LTO of
vehicles with expired
registration; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) determine the
nature of the unreconciled SL
accounts; and (ii) strengthen
the controls on the handling and
recording procedures of PPE by
performing a periodic
reconciliation of the records of
the accounting, property/treasury
and actual inventory results.
observations.managementAction:
On - going process for one time cleansing of PPE.
Will update the PCs.
All vehicle registrations and insurance policies have been verified.
Some vehicles are unregistered and unserviceable/are now operational.
Municipal Accountant to take – up the necessary accounting entries to drop from the books of accounts the amounts lumped under the unreconciled SL and to ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the GL; and recognize in the books of accounts the donated assets received in accordance with Section 36 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.reason: Processing of registrations and insurance registration have been verified. Most of those unregistered are now registered, and those who were unregistered are now insured with GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal
Accountant to
(a) strictly comply with the
required documentation of
transactions prior to
recognition of claims; (b) exert
effort to establish the validity
of the claims of creditors and
their employees to the agency;
and (c) revert to the
unappropriated surplus of the
general fund those accounts
that had remained outstanding
for two years or more and
against which no actual claim
has been filed.
observations.managementAction:
There are some vouchers booked as 2022 accounts payable that are still subject to transaction verifications and validations.
The MTO attended trainings focused on examining books of accounts and to enhance skills in this area in order to equip them for the said activity and implement the said activity this year. BPLO and MTO conducted two meetings with all 13 business establishments and they all agreed to pay the discrepancy on their business permit fees until December 31, 2024.
observations.reason: There are some vouchers booked as 2022 accounts payable that are still subject to transaction verifications and validations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the (a) Bids and
Awards Committee, Municipal
Engineering Office and
Municipal Accountant to
provide the AT with the above-
identified missing procurement
and technical documents to
allow the AT to determine the
appropriate audit action on said
transactions; (b) Municipal
Accountant to ensure that
future claims against government
funds are adequately
documented; and (c) Municipal
Planning and Development
Officer to obtain reasonable
assurance on the viability of
future projects before funds are
committed thereto to preclude
various reprogramming and to
ensure overall efficiency in the
management of government
funds.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant ensures that proper and complete documents are attached to respective vouchers. Municipal Planning and Development Officer to obtain reasonable assurance on viability of projects.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant ensures that proper and complete documents are attached to respective vouchers. Not all missing procurement and technical documents were provided.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the MDRRM Officer,
in collaboration with MDRRMC,
to (a) ensure that the use of
LDRRMF is maximized; (b)
identify and incorporate, under a
separate caption in the
LDRRMFIP the projects,
programs, and activities that will
be charged against the
unexpended LDRRMF from
previous years; and (c) update
or revise the LDRRMFIP as
necessary to effect any changes
in the PPAs during the year.
observations.managementAction: MDRRM Officer to ensure use of LDRRMF is maximized; identify and incorporate projects charged against unexpended LDRRMF; update/revise LDRRMFIP as necessary.
observations.reason: Preparation for the establishment of LGU owned sanitary landfill is on-going. Update of LDRRMFIP not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the GFPS to (a) continue
its efforts in the mainstreaming
of GAD in local operations and
governance to achieve full
compliance with the provisions
of relevant laws and
regulations; and (b) create GAD
database to ensure that priority
gender issues within the locality
are identified and addressed
accordingly.
observations.managementAction: CBMS data gathering on-going in partnership with PSA. GFPS to continue mainstreaming of GAD and complete creation of GAD database.
observations.reason: On-going process; awaiting PSA approval for final data sharing; GAD database not yet fully established.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE
agreed to enforce the immediate
settlement of the remaining
disallowances and strictly
adhere to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in
the processing and documentation
of transactions to avoid further
suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance of ₱14,463.15 was settled during the 1st quarter of CY 2024. An appeal was filed with COA RO for the remaining disallowance amounting to ₱274,090.55.
observations.reason: An appeal was filed with COA RO for the remaining disallowance amounting to ₱274,090.55.

observations.recommendation: 1.8 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed through the Municipal Accountant to cancel the stale checks and restore the balances of the corresponding cash in bank and liability accounts.

2.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure strict compliance with their respective duties and responsibilities to accurately assess the Real Property Taxes; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) check the Real Property Tax (RPT) collections from CY 2023 to present; and (ii) reconcile the RPT Receivable and prepare necessary adjustments as warranted.

3.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Inventory Committee to (i) conduct the semestral physical count of inventories; and (ii) submit the RPCI in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each commodity/stock; (ii) compute the ending inventory and cost of supplies using the moving average method; and (iii) record the cost of issued Semi-Expendable Inventories and Other Supplies and Materials Inventory totaling ₱6,910,038; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain Stock Cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; (ii) consolidate monthly the RIS for supplies and materials using the SSMI; and (iii) submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the Municipal Accountant.

4.25 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain updated PCs; (ii) register with the LTO the vehicles that have expired registration; and (iii) obtain insurance coverage from GSIS; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) take – up the necessary accounting entries to drop from the books of accounts the amounts lumped under the unreconciled SL and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs/SLs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in the GL; and (ii) recognize in the books of accounts the donated assets received in accordance with Section 36 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

5.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly comply with the required documentation of transactions prior to recognition of claims; and (b) exert effort to establish the validity of the claims of creditors.

6.46 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) officials concerned to initiate the implementation of projects funded by the DILG which were not yet started as at year-end; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) refund the balance of ₱39,610 from completed project to the DENR or utilize the fund as instructed; (ii) reclassify collections from services provided by the Municipality thru the Municipal Civil Registry Office on the use of BREQS which were recorded in the trust fund books of the Municipality to the general fund; and (iii) continue the strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust funds.

7.13 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal HRMO to continue updating and reconciling leave credits of employees of the Municipality; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) present both current and non-current portions of the Leave Benefits Payable account in accordance with IPSAS 1 and 39.

8.15 We recommended and the LCE through the (a) BPLO (i) utilize the contracts and/or Purchase Orders in assessing the business taxes of entities that have transactions with the Municipality; (ii) mandate all business taxpayers to submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR); and (iii) henceforth, strictly enforce the Revenue Code of Bongabon; (b) Municipal Treasurer examine and verify the books of accounts and pertinent records of business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer verify, assess, and validate the business taxes filed and paid by the 14 cited business establishments.

9.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to sustain the Municipality's effective use of the 20% Development Fund and its compliance with DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020 by (i) strengthening planning and monitoring mechanisms to ensure timely and full implementation of all programmed projects, including those carried over from previous years; (ii) enhancing project evaluation and prioritization processes to ensure alignment with community needs to maximize developmental impact; and (iii) strictly adhering to approval and reprogramming protocols, such as securing SB Resolutions, to uphold transparency and accountability in fund management.

10.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Agriculture Office to continue monitoring the operations of the (a) eight subprojects for the timely detection of any concerns and delivery of proper interventions, if warranted; and (b) cold storage facility to ensure that it is optimally utilized for the benefit of the farmers.

11.19 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) MDRRM Officer to update or revise the LDRRMFIP as necessary to effect any changes in the PPAs during the year; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the obligated expenditures totaling ₱0.31M; (ii) ensure the accuracy and completeness of financial reports; and (iii) ensure the completeness of supporting documents before payments are made.

12.16 We recommended that the LCE agreed through the GFPS to (a) continue its efforts in the mainstreaming of GAD in local operations and governance to achieve full compliance with GAD laws and regulations; and (b) complete the creation of GAD database to ensure that priority gender issues within the locality are identified and addressed accordingly.

13.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of taxes.
14.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

15.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

16.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the deduction and remittance of the members’ contributions and loan amortizations to Pag-IBIG.

17.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to strictly adhere to the accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the
Local Chief Executive (LCE)
instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to (a) determine the
nature of the existing disparity
in the year-end balance of Cash in Local Treasury as reflected
in their respective records; and
(b) henceforth ensure that the
cash book and ledger balances
of cash accounts are reconciled
at the end of every quarter.
observations.managementAction:
Adjustments were recorded in the year-end balance of Cash in the books.
Conducts quarterly reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the (a) MENRO to
submit to the accounting office
the requisite documents to
facilitate the liquidation of the
remaining funds received from
DENR; (b) officials concerned to
initiate the implementation of
projects funded by DOE, DILG,
BFP and PhilHealth; and (c)
Municipal Accountant to (i)
continue the strict compliance
with the guidelines on the
receipt and utilization of trust
funds; (ii) revert/return to source
agencies the fund for which the
intended purpose could no
longer be pursued or was no
longer needed; and (iii) prepare
the request for write-off of the
unreconciled old accounts in
accordance with the procedures
prescribed.
observations.managementAction:
MENRO to submit documents to Accounting Office.
Officials concerned initiated implementation of projects funded by DOE, DILG, BFP and PhilHealth.
Municipal Accountant to continue strict compliance with guidelines on receipt and utilization of trust funds; revert/return funds when necessary; prepare request for write-off of unreconciled old accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal
Agriculture Office to (a) continue
monitoring the operations of
the eight subprojects for the
timely detection of any concerns
and delivery of proper
interventions, if warranted; (b)
make representation with the
PRDP Regional Office for a
facilitated action on the SWA and
other documentary requirements
for the release of the final
payment for the cold storage
facility; and (c) henceforth,
ensure that said facility is
optimally utilized for the benefit
of the target farmer
beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: MAO to continue monitoring the operations of the eight subprojects; facilitated actions with PRDP Regional Office for final payment; ensure optimal utilization of cold storage facility.
observations.reason: On-going finalization of PPP agreement and operation and management plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the
LCE instruct the MENRO to
(a) develop measures and
sanctions that will ensure the
adherence with the provisions of
R.A. No. 9003 that no open
dumps shall be established; (b)
closely monitor the area where
open dumps are located and the
subsequent clearing of residual
wastes to ensure that the threat
it posed to the community is
eliminated; and (c) continue to
implement strategies on waste
minimization to eventually be
able to generate income from
the same.
observations.managementAction: MENRO to develop measures and sanctions, closely monitor open dump areas and implement strategies on waste minimization.
observations.reason: Preparation for establishment of LGU owned sanitary landfill is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
(a) instruct the BPLO to (i)
utilize the contracts and
Purchase Orders in assessing
the business tax on entities that
had transactions in the tax
computation; (ii) mandate all
business taxpayers to submit a
certified copy of their Income
Tax Return (ITR); (iii) enforce
sanction on EM Gas Station for
operating without an approved
business permit; and (iv)
henceforth strictly enforce the
Revenue Code of Bongabon; (b)
direct the Municipal Treasurer to
examine and verify the books of
accounts and pertinent records
of business establishments; and
(c) task both the BPLO and the
Municipal Treasurer to verify,
assess, and validate the
disclosed under-collection of
business taxes from thirteen (13)
business establishments.
observations.managementAction: BPLO and MTO are now utilizing record of the Municipality as basis on business transactions in the tax computation; BPLO to mandate taxpayers to submit copy of their ITR; MTO attended trainings; BPLO and MTO conducted two meetings with all 13 business establishments and they all agreed to pay the discrepancy.
observations.reason: BPLO and MTO conducted meetings and agreed collection plan; some actions (enforcement, sanctions) remain to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to
continuously comply with the
rules and regulations set by the
BIR on the withholding and
timely remittance of all taxes
due for the period.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will continuously remit taxes withheld on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to continuously
comply with the requirements
of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS
Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will remit all GSIS contributions and premium on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE
instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to continuously
comply with the National
Health Insurance Act by
remitting all contributions of
the members to the Philippine
Health Insurance Corporation.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will remit all PhilHealth contributions on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE
agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to
continuously comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 9679
or the Home Development
Mutual Fund Law of 2009.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer will remit all Pag-IBIG contributions and loan amortizations on time.

RPCPPE was submitted.


















No documents to support the lump sum amounts of items recorded in the PPE.

Reconciliations were made between the actual inventory results and the records maintained.


Receivables were recorded.



Still in the process of completing the procedures for the “one-time” cleansing.

Forwarded the Certified List of Taxpayers to the Accounting Office


















RPT and SET Receivables were recorded based on MTO report.
The LGU continues to do vaccination activities.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct
the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (a) determine the
nature of the existing disparity in the yearend balance of Cash in Local
Treasury as reflected in their respective records; and (b) henceforth ensure
that the cash book and ledger balances of cash accounts are reconciled at the
end of every quarter.

2.7 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant
to cancel the stale checks and restore the balances of the corresponding cash
in bank and liability accounts.

3.23 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Treasurer to (i) update the Property Cards based on the result of the physical
count; (ii) issue demand letters to each accountable officer/personnel for the
production of the missing PPE he/she is accountable for; (iii) investigate the
causes of the missing PPE items to identify the persons deemed liable
therefor; (iv) locate the LTO Certificate of Registration which were not yet
presented for verification of the AT; and (v) cause the renewal of registration
with the LTO of vehicles with expired registration; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) determine the nature of the unreconciled SL accounts; and
(ii) strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures of PPE
by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting,
property/treasury and actual inventory results.

4.10 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to (a) strictly comply with the required documentation of transactions prior
to recognition of claims; (b) exert effort to establish the validity of the claims
of creditors and their employees to the agency; and (c) revert to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund those accounts that had
remained outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual
claim has been filed.

5.40 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MENRO to submit
to the accounting office the requisite documents to facilitate the liquidation
of the remaining funds received from DENR; (b) officials concerned to
initiate the implementation of projects funded by DOE, DILG, BFP and
PhilHealth; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) continue the strict
compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust funds;
(ii) revert/return to source agencies the fund for which the intended purpose
could no longer be pursued or was no longer needed; and (iii) prepare the
request for write-off of the unreconciled old accounts in accordance with the
procedures prescribed.

6.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Bids and Awards
Committee, Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Accountant to
provide the AT with the above-identified missing procurement and technical
documents to allow the AT to determine the appropriate audit action on said
transactions; (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that future claims against
government funds are adequately documented; and (c) Municipal Planning
and Development Officer to obtain reasonable assurance on the viability of
future projects before funds are committed thereto to preclude various
reprogramming and to ensure overall efficiency in the management of
government funds.

7.16 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Agriculture
Office to (a) continue monitoring the operations of the eight subprojects for
the timely detection of any concerns and delivery of proper interventions, if
warranted; (b) make representation with the PRDP Regional Office for a
facilitated action on the SWA and other documentary requirements for the
release of the final payment for the cold storage facility; and (c) henceforth,
ensure that said facility is optimally utilized for the benefit of the target
farmer beneficiaries.

8.17 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MDRRM Officer, in
collaboration with MDRRMC, to (a) ensure that the use of LDRRMF is
maximized; (b) identify and incorporate, under a separate caption in the
LDRRMFIP the projects, programs, and activities that will be charged
against the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years; and (c) update or
revise the LDRRMFIP as necessary to effect any changes in the PPAs during
the year.

9.28 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the MENRO to (a) develop
measures and sanctions that will ensure the adherence with the provisions of
R.A. No. 9003 that no open dumps shall be established; (b) closely monitor
the area were open dumps is located and the subsequent clearing of residual
wastes to ensure that the threat it posed to the community is eliminated; and
(c) continue to implement strategies on waste minimization to eventually be
able to generate income from the same.

10.18 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) instruct the BPLO to (i) utilize
the contracts and Purchase Orders in assessing business tax on entities that
had transactions in the Municipality; (ii) mandate all business taxpayers to
submit a certified copy of their Income Tax Return (ITR); (iii) enforce
sanction on EM Gas Station for operating without an approved business
permit; and (iv) henceforth strictly enforce the Revenue Code of Bongabon;
(b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of
accounts and pertinent records of business establishments; and (c) task both
the BPLO and the Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate the
disclosed under-collection of business taxes from thirteen (13) business
establishments.

11.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) continue
its efforts in the mainstreaming of GAD in local operations and governance
to achieve full compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and
regulations; and (b) create GAD database to ensure that priority gender
issues within the locality are identified and addressed accordingly.

12.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the rules and
regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of all
taxes due for the period.

13.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of
R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

14.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the National Health
Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members to the
Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

15.4 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurer to continuously comply with the requirements of
R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

16.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to enforce the immediate settlement
of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of
transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

observations.managementAction: Vaccination drives were conducted.
Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were submitted.
Information and education campaign were conducted.
Continues to comply with the rules and regulations set by the BIR on the withholding and timely remittance of all taxes due.

observations.recommendation:
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the MHO to determine and recognize in the
books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end and to
henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same; (b) MHO to
continue spearheading the LGU’s vaccination roll out until the desired local
herd immunity is attained; and (c) BAC Chairman and MHO to prepare and
submit the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 in future
procurements of ancillary supplies and services.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue adhering with laws, rules
and regulations pertinent to the grant of Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special
Risk Allowance.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) MHO to continue
the COVID operations, particularly the vaccination program; and (b)
Municipal Accountant to continue the close monitoring of the utilization of
COVID funds to ensure compliance with pertinent fiscal regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to ensure (a)
the immediate communication with the new Chairman of the Pinagbuklod
PMPC to establish the actual status of the two (2) hand tractors that were
not presented; and (b) the Municipality’s close monitoring of the I-Reap
PRDP subprojects to guarantee its continuous success and the timely
provision of help and intervention should the Proponent Group have a need
for it.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer
in coordination with the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count
of PPEs and render the requisite report; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) update
the Property Cards based on the result of the physical count; (ii) initiate the
proper disposition of the missing items in collaboration with the PNP office
concerned, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges against the
persons accountable, as warranted; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) endeavor
to determine the details of the PPE accounts presented in lump sums; (ii)
coordinate with the MTO for the reconciliation of property records; (iii)
recognize the receivables for the missing items and (iii) prepare the documents
that will be needed for the request of “one-time” PPE cleansing as guided in
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting
documents to ascertain the nature/particulars of the dormant accounts and
determine the legality of said claims. Likewise, if efforts exerted become
futile, management should secure an authority from the COA to write off the
subject dormant accounts supported by the requirements prescribed in COA
Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We also recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC members
and the Municipal Engineer to continuously plan its PPAs as well as provide
a reasonable timetable for all procurement and implementation of future
development projects; and (b) Municipal Accountant to submit the requisite
documents for further verification/validation of the transactions.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to: (a) ensure strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization
of trust funds; (b) revert/return to source agencies the fund for which the
intended purpose could no longer be pursued or was no longer needed; and (c)
prepare the request for write-off of the unreconciled old accounts.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the: (a) Chairman, Bids and
Awards Committee to (i) ensure that the necessary procurement information
are timely posted in the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB)
portal and PhilGEPS utilizing the Municipality’s website in the posting of
procurement opportunities and information; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i)
initiate the return of unspent financial assistance to the NGA concerned; (ii)
ensure that all requisite documents are attached to the disbursement
vouchers; (c) LDRRM Officer and the Local Finance Committee to cause the
submission of the requisite reports for LDRRMF and financial assistance to
oversight and donor agencies, respectively.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to (a) update the
GAD database to include sex-disaggregated data; (b) administer the HGDG
test whenever they use attribution in the budgeting phase and
accomplishment report preparation; and (c) seek help from experts and
include HGDG training in the budget to ensure the correctness of the GAD
issues identified and the responsiveness of the corresponding GAD PPAs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue complying with the
provisions of R.A. No. 9003; (b) instruct the MENRO to (i) develop measures
that will ensure the strict implementation of the “No Segregation, No
Collection Policy; and (ii) closely monitor the closure of the open dumpsite
and the subsequent clearing of residual wastes to ensure that the threat it
posed to the community is eliminated.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with the rules
and regulations set by the BIR to timely remit all taxes withheld.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to continuously comply with the
National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions of the members
to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to continuously comply with the
requirements of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law
of 2009.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to enforce the immediate settlement
of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and
auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of
transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

(a) exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting documents to ascertain the validity of said accounts; 


(b) recognize impairment losses on these accounts, as determined; and 



(c) consider the option of requesting for authority from COA for the writing off of said receivables in accordance with the prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction:
No action taken

No action taken

Request for write-off were prepared and submitted for review.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: Management continuously complies with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) send demand letters for the settlement of the outstanding cash advances and take appropriate legal action in case of unjustified non-compliance therewith; and (ii) strictly observe the guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances; and (b) provide the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the Liquidation Reports reportedly submitted to COA in CY 2015, and any credit notice that may have been issued therefor.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) BAC members and the Municipal Engineer to continue planning for and timely schedule all procurement and implementation of future development projects; and (b) Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to regularly monitor the status of project implementation to allow timely interventions against possible delays.

We recommended and management concurred to (a) revisit the provisions of PCW/DILG/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 pertaining to the chargeable and non-chargeable expenses to GAD Budget; and (b) comply with the provisions of DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 to institutionalize a GAD Database to enable the management to prepare GPB that is more effective and gender-responsive.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) continue adhering with the provisions of R.A. 10121 or the PDRRM Act of 2010, and all other rules and regulations pertaining to the utilization of LDRRMF; (b) develop PPAs that will help mitigate the risk of vulnerability to calamities; and (c) update the LDRRMC organizational chart to establish more reliable information that distinguish the roles of each members.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) revisit their NSWMC approved 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan, particularly Section 11.3, Chapter XI on the targeted revenue collections from recycling and sale of recyclable materials; (b) provide policy on the disposition of processed products from MRF and sale of recyclable materials; and (c) tap other income sources to support SWM activities of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to (a) develop, recommend and strictly implement measures to enhance income collections; and (b) ensure that strict collection measures are religiously observed.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management concurred to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed to enforce the immediate settlement of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended and the Management agreed  that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

(a) Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly certified list of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year 2018; and

(b) Municipal Accountant to
:
        (i) set up RPT and SET Receivables for each year corresponding to the lists and make necessary adjustments as warranted; and

        (ii) henceforth, observe the prescribed accounting procedures in recording RPT and SET receivable and income accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:

(a) enforce the strict compliance of the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances; and

(b) monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period
.
observations.managementAction: The Management directed the department managers to strictly observe rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.reason: Some cash advances granted were not liquidated within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the Due from other Funds and Due to other Funds accounts to:

(a) identify the cause/s of the discrepancy/ies in the balances of the two accounts; and

(b) effect the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Management has conducted reconciliation of balances and has been monitoring the same to avoid discrepancies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

(a) examine and review the supporting documents of dormant accounts and communicate with the payees for possible payment of the claims if the transactions were found valid and regular; and

(b) strictly comply with Section 98 of PD 1445 on the reversion of accounts payables which are outstanding for two or more years.
observations.managementAction: The Management is identifying which of the claims were valid and already contacting the payees for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the trial balances and financial statements for each economic enterprise of the Municipality at the end of the year to ensure availability of adequate information on their operations for decision making purposes in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the R.A. No. 7160 and NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the:

(a) Municipal Development Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms; and

(b) to require the Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund for eligible expenditures only.

We recommended and the Management concurred to:

(a) refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations to have sufficient funds for calamity purposes; and

(b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to fully utilize the GAD funds in order to attain the desired benefits that could be derived therefrom. Moreover, strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.reason: GAD database were not properly established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to rigorously implement the No Segregation No Collection Policy and impose sanctions to noncompliant, if necessary.
observations.reason: This ordinance was still not fully implemented to all barangays. It was informed that citation tickets will be issued to noncompliant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

(a) impose strong internal control on the remittances and require the field collectors to remit the collections daily or the next working day; and

(b) prepare the CRAAF and require all revenue collectors to prepare the RAAF and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
We recommended and the Management concurred to continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
We recommended the Management to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.reason: The BAC justified that due to the ratio of work volume against number of people in their office, PMR was not prepared. However, they assured the audit team that this will be done for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management on the substantial compliance on the guidelines set forth by DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in appropriation and utilization of its Development Fund. 

However, proper monitoring should be done by the concerned personnel in order to fully attain the desired socio-economic and environmental benefit of the fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 6 of Part II – Observations and Recommendations

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) enforce the strict compliance of the guidelines provided in Items 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of P.D. 1445 in the liquidation of their cash advances; and (b) monitor the liquidation and demand settlement if no liquidation was submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) examine and review the supporting documents of dormant accounts and communicate with the payees for possible payment of the claims if the transactions were found valid and regular; and (b)strictly comply with Section 98 of PD 1445 on the reversion of accounts payables which are outstanding for two or more years.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the trial balances and financial statements for each economic enterprise of the Municipality at the end of the year to ensure availability of adequate information on their operations for decision making purposes in compliance with the pertinent provisions of the R.A. No. 7160 and NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the: (a) Municipal Development Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms; and (b) to require the Budget Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning Development Officer to strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund for eligible expenditures only.

We recommended and the Management concurred to: (a) refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, and response and rescue operations to have sufficient funds for calamity purposes; and (b) require the concerned MDRRM Officer to comply with the reporting guidelines on the preparation and regular submission to the COA Auditor of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of Funds prepared by the Municipal Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation.

We commend the Management on its effort on the substantial compliance with R.A. No. 9003 and recommend to carry on its determination on implementing the same and its Solid Waste Management Plan to continually achieve the protection of the environment and general welfare of the constituents. Moreover, we recommended and the Management agreed to rigorously implement the No Segregation No Collection Policy and impose sanctions to noncompliant, if necessary.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) impose strong internal control on the remittances and require the field collectors to remit the collections daily or the next working day; and (b) prepare the CRAAF and require all revenue collectors to prepare the RAAF and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

No. 7160.

However, proper monitoring should be done by the concerned personnel in order to fully attain the desired socio-economic and environmental benefit of the fund.

We recommended and the Management concurred that the GAD Focal Point System, in coordination with the Local Planning Development Office, create an organized GAD database to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as support to the evidence-based GAD planning, programming and policy making pursuant to PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and R.A. No. 9710.

observations.reason: The Management reasoned out that everyday travel is not practical due to far distance between the Municipality and the bank.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

a) ensure that all transactions are recorded promptly in the cashbook to establish the actual balance of cash held by her at the end of the day; and

b) record the cash advances with specific details to properly establish the balances, and reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting documents to ascertain the nature/particulars of the dormant accounts and determine the legality of said claims and payables.

Likewise, if efforts exerted
become futile, management should secure an authority from the COA to write off of the subject dormant accounts supported by the requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: The dormant accounts receivable cannot be located anymore. The Municipal Accountant requested a write-off of these dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management:

exert effort to locate the titles, deed of donations or deed of absolute sale of the other lands listed in the Summary of Owned Lands provided by the Office of the Assessor, to properly safeguard the interest of the government.

take immediate action to ensure that the immediate registration and titling of the subject parcels of land in the name of the municipality are undertaken.

allocate funds for incidental expenses to be incurred in the consolidation of titles.



regularly inspect the site of the subject parcel of land must be conducted to deter possible unlawful tenants from occupying the said land owned by the municipality.

require the reconciliation of the records and documents on file between the Offices of Accounting, Treasurer’s and Assessor’s should be done regularly to ascertain that the balance recorded in the books of the LGU is valid and accurate.
observations.reason:
No funds available yet

Allocated funds are insufficient to cover all the incidental expenses in the consolidation of titles.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed on the following courses of actions:

Property Cards should be prepared and updated by the Municipal Treasurer in details in order to closely monitor the acquired assets of the Municipality in compliance with Section 119 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume I;

exert efforts in locating the missing Certificate of Registration (CRs) to establish ownership over the motor vehicles.  In the event that no CRs could be located within the municipality, the Municipal Treasurer should secure a notarized affidavit of loss and make a representation to the Land Transportation Office for the issuance of a duplicate CR; and

Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should regularly reconcile their records in order to immediately detect or correct discrepancies or deficiencies, if any.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant would

coordinate with the concerned offices for the proper and complete documentation of the said payables;

(b) consider reversion of the recorded payables without documents or details and those which are more than two years; and

(c) ensure that the payables recorded are adequately and properly documented.

We recommended and management agreed to:

strictly comply with Section 53 (e) of the IRR of RA 9184.  Procurement of common-use supplies be first referred to the PS-DBM before resorting thru other mode of procurement; and

require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.reason: Lack of sufficient information by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Auditor would be furnished with the required copies of contract/purchase orders and their supporting documents.  

Likewise, notices of deliveries of procured items should be transmitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period for the timely inspection in aid of post audit.

We recommended and the management agreed to continue the provision of specific budget for the Solid Waste Programs and Activities and implement fully and efficiently the prioritized solid waste management projects and activities covered by regular appropriation.
observations.reason: Waste segregation at source is not fully implemented by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the GAD Focal Point/Person 

(a) to continue to comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD and the preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD and 

(b) monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with GAD Plans and Budget as reviewed by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG).

We recommended that the:

Local School Board prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;

management fully complied with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the aforementioned missing documents to fully establish the validity of the claims; and

Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents and ensure that the necessary documents were prepared and checked before the payments are effected.

We recommended that management continue its compliance with the provisions of RA No. 10121 regarding the funding and implementation of program/activities related to disaster risk reduction and management system.
We recommended that management continue its compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 regarding the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that management continue to withhold all taxes due to BIR and to remit the same within the prescribed period in compliance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 of the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
We recommended that management continue to comply with the provisions on withholding and remittance of GSIS premiums.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting documents to ascertain the nature/particulars of the dormant accounts and determine the legality of said claims and payables.

Likewise, if despite all the efforts exerted prove to be futile, management should secure an authority from the COA to write off of the subject dormant accounts supported by the requirements prescribed in COA Circular No.  93-404 dated October 18, 1993 and COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.
observations.managementAction:
Management will continue to exert effort in finding data or documents pertaining to dormant accounts.  



If however, all efforts failed, management will seek approval for the write-off of the said claims.
observations.reason:
Lack of available data or documents found in the Municipality.



Management did not yet submit request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We advised the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from utilizing the Special  Education Fund (SEF) for non-related activities but rather use said fund for purposes for which it was established.

We recommended that management comply with provisions of Section 4 of Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998 of DECS/DBM/DILG regarding proper utilization of Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction:
Management still used the SEF on expenses not included in the allowed per DECS/DBM/DILG JC No. 1.


Management has informed the concerned officials and employees regarding the set-up of budgets for SEF and utilization thereof.
observations.reason:
LGU failed to fully comply due to lack of budget for  schools within the Municipality.

Management failed to fully enforce the provisions of the  circular.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended the following courses of action to address the audit observation noted, to wit:

Management officials concerned should take immediate action to ensure that the immediate registration and titling of the subject parcel of land in the name of the municipality are undertaken.

Funds should be allocated for incidental expenses to be incurred in 

the consolidation of titles.

Regular site inspection of the subject parcel of land must be conducted to deter possible unlawful tenants from occupying the said land owned by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management have taken the records of the Offices of the Treasurer and Assessor to get a relevant information on the lands owned by the municipality.
observations.reason:
No funds were allocated.

Titles are still not yet consolidated

No actual site inspection was done to ensure that land is without unlawful tenants.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive – 

coordinate with the barangay officials to educate its residents in the proper disposal of their respective household wastes and strictly enforce the no segregation/no collection policy;

instruct MENRO to revise the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan of the municipality and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval; and  
  
comply with the pertinent provisions of RA  No. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction:
No seminar or activities to educate residents on proper disposal of wastes.




Management was able to communicate the Solid Waste Management Plan.



Management prepared the necessary documents for solid waste.
observations.reason:
Barangay officials were not properly instructed on proper disposal of waste.







No sanitary landfill established at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the receipt and deposit of collections as well as the preparation and submission of relevant reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer a) ensure that all transactions are recorded promptly in the cashbook to establish the actual balance of cash held by her at the end of the day; and b) record the cash advances with specific details to properly establish the balances, and reconcile the same with the subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert extra efforts to locate pertinent records/schedules and supporting documents to ascertain the nature/particulars of the dormant accounts and determine the legality of said claims and payables.

Likewise, if efforts exerted become futile, management should secure an authority from the COA to write off of the subject dormant accounts supported by the requirements prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the management:
exert effort to locate the titles, deed of donations or deed of absolute sale of the other lands listed in the Summary of Owned Lands provided by the Office of the Assessor, to properly safeguard the interest of the government.
take immediate action to ensure that the immediate registration and titling of the subject parcels of land in the name of the municipality are undertaken.
allocate funds for incidental expenses to be incurred in the consolidation of titles.
regularly inspect the site of the subject parcel of land must be conducted to deter possible unlawful tenants from occupying the said land owned by the municipality.
require the reconciliation of the records and documents on file between the Offices of Accounting, Treasurer’s and Assessor’s should be done regularly to ascertain that the balance recorded in the books of the LGU is valid and accurate.

We recommended and management agreed on the following courses of actions:
Property Cards should be prepared and updated by the Municipal Treasurer in details in order to closely monitor the acquired assets of the Municipality in compliance with Section 119 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume I;
exert efforts in locating the missing Certificate of Registration (CRs) to establish ownership over the motor vehicles.  In the event that no CRs could be located within the municipality, the Municipal Treasurer should secure a notarized affidavit of loss and make a representation to the Land Transportation Office for the issuance of a duplicate CR; and
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should regularly reconcile their records in order to immediately detect or correct discrepancies or deficiencies, if any.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant would (a) coordinate with the concerned offices for the proper and complete documentation of the said payables; (b) consider reversion of the recorded payables without documents or details and those which are more than two years; and (c) ensure that the payables recorded are adequately and properly documented.

We recommended and management agreed to:
strictly comply with Section 53 (e) of the IRR of RA 9184.  Procurement of common-use supplies be first referred to the PS-DBM before resorting thru other mode of procurement; and
require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended and management agreed that the Auditor would be furnished with the required copies of contract/purchase orders and their supporting documents.  Likewise, notices of deliveries of procured items should be transmitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period for the timely inspection in aid of post audit.

We recommended and the management agreed to continue the provision of specific budget for the Solid Waste Programs and Activities and implement fully and efficiently the prioritized solid waste management projects and activities covered by regular appropriation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the GAD Focal Point/Person (a) to continue to comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD and the preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and regulations on GAD and (b) monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with GAD Plans and Budget as reviewed by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG).

We recommended that the:
Local School Board prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute;
management fully complied with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and submit the aforementioned missing documents to fully establish the validity of the claims; and
Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents and ensure that the necessary documents were prepared and checked before the payments are effected.

We recommended that management continue its compliance with the provisions of RA No. 10121 regarding the funding and implementation of program/activities related to disaster risk reduction and management system.
We recommended that management continue its compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 regarding the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that management continue to withhold all taxes due to BIR and to remit the same within the prescribed period in compliance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 of the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
We recommended that management continue to comply with the provisions on withholding and remittance of GSIS premiums.

We recommended and management agreed to enforce the immediate settlement of the remaining disallowances and strictly adhere to the accounting and auditing rules and regulations in the processing and documentation of transactions to avoid further suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee for the conduct of a complete physical inventory and preparation of a properly accomplished physical inventory report duly signed and approved.

Management was also advised to strictly observe the pertinent rules and regulations regarding proper management and control of properties as well as the procedures involve in the conduct of physical inventory taking and rendition of complete and reliable inventory reports.
observations.managementAction:
Management conducted actual physical inventory count of PPE and was able to submit the latest inventory report as of March 31, 2016.


Management was not able to properly reconcile the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We advised the municipal officials concerned to locate the unsubmitted vouchers and supporting documents and effect immediate submission to COA to enable post-audit of said accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to submit the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We advised management to –

cause the immediate submission of all documentary requirements pertaining to the suspended transactions;

instruct the persons liable to settle their respective disallowances; and


comply with existing rules and regulations regarding payment of overtime services to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We reminded the management that overtime services should not be rendered to finish the backlog if absences are made on regular days.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the concerned employees to submit the necessary documents to comply with the recommendation and lifting of suspensions.  Likewise, refunds were already made by employees with disallowances due to overpayment of overtime pay.
observations.reason: Some employees still failed to comply on the submission of supporting documents for overtime services rendered and refunds for issued notices of  suspension and disallowance, respectively.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
(a) provide for a motor pool wherein unserviceable properties as well as assets still use for operations could be properly secured and  safeguarded against the elements of wear and tear; 

(b) create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of  Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and the immediate disposal of said items; 

(c) prepare the IIRUP for the one (1) unit of 2003 Lancer Advan; and

(d) promptly cause the sale of the unserviceable vehicles thru public auction in accordance COA Circular No. 86-264.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant locate the documents pertaining to the turn-over of the ambulance to the insurance company in order to drop the cost of said vehicle from the books of the municipality.

Management gave assurance that the implementation of the audit recommendations will be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2016, the LGU were able to complete the disposal of some motor vehicles and heavy equipment.  The LGU also submitted the request for inspection for the disposal of the 2nd batch of motor vehicles.  The other unserviceable properties were stored and will be properly tagged for future disposal.
observations.reason: Management prioritized the disposal of the motor vehicles and heavy equipment since it took so much space in the municipal hall’s compound.  The 3rd batch of proposed disposal of unserviceable assets was being prepared as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue to withhold all taxes due to BIR and to remit the same within the prescribed period in compliance with Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 of the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to remit the withheld taxes on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that persons liable settle/refund immediately the   outstanding suspensions/disallowances as of  December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: Refund was made on the disallowance.  While one (1) Appeal was already filed for the disallowance of payment of LGUGC fees.
observations.reason: The ND No. 2014-001-100 (13) is under appeal at COA Regional Office No. III.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that to continue its compliance with the provisions of RA No. 10121 regarding the funding and implementation of program/activities related to support the municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management system.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply with the provisions of RA No. 10121.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continue to comply with the provisions of RA 8291 regarding the proper deductions of GSIS premiums from its employees and its timely remittance to GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Management continued to comply with the remittance of mandatory contributions.
